## Enhance Code Emphasis

The `enhanceCodeEmphasis` source enhancer adds visual emphasis to specific lines in code examples by marking them with a `data-hl` attribute. This allows you to highlight important code patterns or call attention to specific sections in demos using simple comment annotations.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Basic Usage
  - Comment Patterns
    - Single Line Emphasis
    - Single Line with Description
    - Strong Emphasis
    - Multi-line Emphasis
    - Multi-line with Description
    - Text Highlighting
    - Multiple Text Highlighting
  - Configuration
    - Adding to Source Enhancers
    - Comment Prefix
  - Comment Syntax
    - Supported Formats
    - Description Format
    - Displaying Comments in Documentation
  - Advanced Patterns
    - Multiple Single Lines
    - Nested Multi-line Blocks

  - Frame Reframing
    - How It Works
    - Frame Data Attributes
    - Configurable Padding
    - Per-Directive Padding with `@padding N`
    - Per-Directive Focus Max with `@min N`
    - The `@focus` Directive
    - Standalone `@focus` (Focus Without Highlight)
    - Styling Frame Types
    - Indent Shifting
    - Collapsible Code Block
  - Styling Emphasized Lines
    - Frame-Level Highlighting
    - Line-Level Highlighting
    - Showing Descriptions
  - Best Practices
    - Be Specific
    - Use Descriptions
    - Group Related Lines
    - Match Comment Style
  - Troubleshooting
    - Lines Not Being Emphasized

- Exports:

## Enhance Code Inline

A rehype plugin that improves the visual appearance of inline `<code>` elements by wrapping HTML/JSX tag patterns in styled spans and reclassifying misidentified tokens.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Wrapper Classes
    - Key Features
  - Installation
  - Usage
    - Basic Usage
    - With transformHtmlCodeInline
  - Transformation Examples
    - HTML Entity Tags (pl-ent → di-ht)
    - JSX Component Tags (pl-c1 → di-jt)
    - Closing Tags
    - Multiple Consecutive Tags
    - Tags with Attributes
  - How It Works
    - Pattern Matching
    - Processing Flow
    - Queue-Based Processing
  - Edge Cases
    - Self-Closing Tags
    - Code Blocks
    - Missing Brackets
    - Token Reclassification
    - Built-in Type Enhancement (TypeScript only)
  - Why This Enhancement?
  - Plugin Order
  - Related

</details>

## Enhance Code Types

A rehype plugin that transforms code identifiers into clickable links, allowing users to navigate to type documentation by clicking on type references in code snippets.

## HAST Utilities

The `hastUtils` module provides utilities for converting between HAST (Hypertext Abstract Syntax Tree) nodes, strings, and React JSX elements. These utilities are essential for processing syntax-highlighted code and converting markdown/HTML structures to React components.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Usage
  - API
    - `hastOrJsonToJsx`
    - `hastToJsx`
    - `stringOrHastToJsx`
    - `stringOrHastToString`
  - Additional Types
  - When to Use
- Exports:
  - buildDictionary
    - Parameters: textContent
  - compressHast
    - Parameters: json, textContent
  - compressHastAsync
    - Parameters: input, textContent
  - computeDictionaryChecksum
    - Parameters: dict
  - decompressHast
    - Parameters: base64, textContent
  - decompressHastAsync
    - Parameters: base64, textContent
  - getHastTextContent
    - Parameters: node
  - getShallowTextContent
    - Parameters: element
  - HastDictionaryMismatchError
  - hastOrJsonToJsx
    - Parameters: hastOrJson, components
  - hastToJsx
    - Parameters: hast, components
  - stringOrHastToJsx
    - Parameters: source, highlighted, components
  - stringOrHastToString
    - Parameters: source
  - stripHighlightingSpans
    - Parameters: root
- Types: CHECKSUM_BYTES, HAST_DICTIONARY, MAX_DICTIONARY_SIZE

</details>

## Lint JavaScript Demo Focus

An ESLint rule that automatically inserts `@focus` comments around the preview section of demo files. These comments control which parts of the code are focused when rendered in documentation.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Installation & Usage
    - With `wrapReturn`
  - How It Works
    - 1\. Wrapper elements
    - 2\. Non-wrapper returns
    - 3\. Function body highlighting
  - Named Export Detection
  - Types
- Types: lintJavascriptDemoFocus

## Load Isomorphic Code Source

The `createLoadIsomorphicCodeSource` factory builds a [`LoadSource`](../load-isomorphic-code-variant/page.mdx) function from two injected primitives — `fetchSource` and `resolveImports` — making it the platform-independent core for loading and analyzing JavaScript/TypeScript/CSS source files.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Usage
  - Injection Points
    - `fetchSource`
    - `resolveImports`
  - Import Storage Modes
    - `'flat'` (default)
    - `'canonical'`
    - `'import'`
  - Return Value
  - Comment Processing
    - `removeCommentsWithPrefix`
    - `notableCommentsPrefix`
  - How It Works
  - Examples
    - Browser loader against an HTTP source tree
    - Loading a JavaScript File
    - Loading with External Dependencies
    - Wrapping with a cache
  - When to Use
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createLoadIsomorphicCodeSource
    - Parameters: options
- Types: IsomorphicImports, LoadIsomorphicCodeSourceOptions

</details>

## Load Isomorphic Code Variant

The `loadIsomorphicCodeVariant` module provides utilities for loading, processing, and transforming code variants with support for syntax highlighting, TypeScript-to-JavaScript transformation, extra files, and metadata management.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:

## Load Precomputed Code Highlighter

The precompute loader is a Webpack/Turbopack loader that enables build-time optimization of code examples by processing demo files and precomputing syntax highlighting, TypeScript transformations, and dependency resolution.

<summary>Outline</summary>

[Read more](./load-precomputed-code-highlighter/page.mdx)

## Load Precomputed Code Highlighter Client

The precomputed code client loader is a Webpack/Turbopack loader that enables build-time optimization of client-side demo components by processing demo client files and precomputing external dependencies for live editing environments.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Configuration
    - Recommended: withDocsInfra Plugin
    - Manual Next.js Setup
    - File Structure
  - Usage
    - Basic Demo Client File
    - With Options
    - Multiple Variants
    - Custom Client Factory Functions
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Parse Factory Call
    - 2\. Resolve Variant Paths
    - 3\. Collect Externals
    - 4\. Inject Externals
  - Output Structure
    - Externals Format
  - Error Handling
    - Single Client Factory Function per File
    - Missing Variant Files
    - Invalid Function Signature
    - Skip Precompute
  - Benefits
    - Build-time Externals Resolution
    - Live Editing Support
    - Performance
  - Best Practices
    - File Organization
    - Client Factory Design
    - Configuration Tips
  - Related Documentation

</details>

## Load Precomputed Sitemap

The precomputed sitemap loader is a Webpack/Turbopack loader that processes markdown files at build time to create optimized search indexes and navigation data. It extracts metadata from documentation pages and generates structured sitemap data with Orama search schema.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Configuration
    - Recommended: withDocsInfra Plugin
    - Manual Next.js Setup
    - File Structure
  - Usage
    - Basic Sitemap File
    - Skip Precomputation
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Parse Factory Call
    - 2\. Extract Path Metadata
    - 3\. Parse Markdown Files
    - 4\. Optimize Data
    - 5\. Generate Schema
    - 6\. Replace Factory Call
  - Output Structure
  - Orama Integration
  - Path-Based Metadata
    - Path Segment to Title
    - URL Prefix Generation
  - Benefits
    - Build-Time Processing
    - Search Optimization
    - Hot Reload Support
  - Best Practices
    - File Organization
    - Metadata Quality
    - Performance
  - Performance Tracking
    - Build-Time Optimization
    - Bundle Size Reduction
    - Performance Logging
  - Error Handling
    - Missing Files
    - Parse Errors
    - Single Factory Function
  - Related

</details>

## Load Precomputed Types

The precomputed types loader is a Webpack/Turbopack loader that automatically generates comprehensive type documentation for your TypeScript components at build time. Write your components in TypeScript, and the loader extracts props, methods, and type signatures—ready to display in your documentation.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Usage
    - Basic Types File
    - Using Types in Pages
    - Multiple Components
    - Skipping Precomputation
  - Configuration
    - Recommended: withDocsInfra Plugin
    - Manual Next.js Setup
    - File Structure
  - Options Reference
    - Loader Options
  - Types
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Parse Factory Call
    - 2\. Delegate to loadServerTypes
    - 3\. Insert Precompute Value
    - 4\. Rewrite Component Imports
    - 5\. Track Dependencies
  - Related
- Exports:
  - default
    - Parameters: source
- Types: LoaderOptions

[Read more](./load-precomputed-types/page.mdx)

## Load Server Code Meta

The `loadServerCodeMeta` utility parses demo files to extract variant information and resolves file paths for code metadata. It reads demo index files, finds `createDemo` factory calls, and builds a `Code` object mapping variant names to their file URLs.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Single Variant Demo
    - Multiple Variants Demo
    - Named Exports
  - Advanced Usage
    - Custom Implementation
    - Integration with CodeHighlighter
  - How It Works
  - When to Use
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createLoadServerCodeMeta
    - Parameters: \_options
  - DirectoryReader
    - Parameters: path
  - loadServerCodeMeta
    - Parameters: url
  - resolveImportResultWithFs
    - Parameters: importResult, options
  - resolveModulePathsWithFs
    - Parameters: moduleUrls, options
  - resolveModulePathWithFs
    - Parameters: modulePath, options, includeTypeDefs
  - resolveVariantPathsWithFs
    - Parameters: variants, options
- Types: CreateLoadCodeMetaOptions, DirectoryEntry, ResolveModulePathOptions

</details>

## Load Server Code Source

The `loadServerCodeSource` utility reads source files from the local filesystem and analyzes their dependencies. It is a thin Node.js wrapper around [`createLoadIsomorphicCodeSource`](../load-isomorphic-code-source/page.mdx) that wires the platform-independent loader to `fs/promises` and to a filesystem-backed module resolver.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Usage
  - What This Wrapper Adds
    - `fetchSource`: Node.js `fs/promises`
    - `resolveImports`: filesystem-backed module resolution
  - When to Use
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createLoadServerCodeSource
    - Parameters: options
  - loadServerCodeSource
    - Parameters: url

</details>

## Load Server Page Index

The `loadServerPageIndex` utility reads a markdown file and extracts page metadata for use in sitemaps. It parses the markdown content, extracts titles, descriptions, sections, and keywords, and derives additional metadata like URL prefix from the file path.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

[Read more](./load-server-page-index/page.mdx)

## Load Server Sitemap

The `loadServerSitemap` utility parses sitemap index files to load page metadata at runtime. It reads the sitemap index file, finds [`createSitemap`](../../factories/create-sitemap/page.mdx) factory calls, resolves page imports, and builds a complete `Sitemap` object with schema and data.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Loading from Sitemap Index
  - Advanced Usage
    - Custom Implementation
    - Integration with Search
  - Comparison: Build-Time vs Runtime
  - Types
    - `loadServerSitemap`
    - `createLoadServerSitemap`
    - `createSitemapSchema`
  - Additional Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createLoadServerSitemap
    - Parameters: options
  - createSitemapSchema
  - loadServerSitemap
    - Parameters: url
  - LoadServerSitemap
    - Parameters: url
- Types: CreateLoadServerSitemapOptions

</details>

## Load Server Types

A server-side function for loading and processing TypeScript types with syntax highlighting. This function coordinates the type extraction pipeline and applies HAST transformations for rendering.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Usage
    - Default: Load from existing types.md
    - With sync: Extract and regenerate types.md
  - When to Use
    - Use `loadServerTypes` when
    - Use `syncTypes` directly when
    - Use `loadServerTypesText` directly when
  - Performance
    - With `sync: false` (default)
    - With `sync: true`
    - Highlighting Overhead
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Load type data
    - 2\. Compute slugs
    - 3\. Apply highlightTypes
    - 4\. Apply highlightTypesMeta
      - Hiding JSDoc Comments
    - 5\. Build anchorMap
  - Related
  - Types
- Exports:
  - loadServerTypes
    - Parameters: options
- Types: HighlightedClassProperty, HighlightedClassTypeMeta, HighlightedComponentTypeMeta, HighlightedEnumMemberMeta, HighlightedFunctionTypeMeta, HighlightedHookTypeMeta, HighlightedMethod, HighlightedParameter, HighlightedProperty, HighlightedRawTypeMeta, HighlightedTypesMeta, LoadServerTypesOptions, LoadServerTypesResult, SerializedHastCompressed, SerializedHastRoot, TypesOutputFormat

</details>

## Load Server Types Meta

The core server-side function for loading and formatting TypeScript type metadata. This function extracts types from your source files and returns structured data ready for documentation generation or rendering.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Usage
    - Basic Usage
    - Multiple Variants
    - Filtering Descriptions
    - Filtering External Types
  - Comparison with Related Functions
  - Performance
    - First Request
    - Subsequent Requests
    - Multi-Process Improvement
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Load TypeScript Configuration
    - 2\. Resolve Library Source Files
    - 3\. Find Meta Files
    - 4\. Process Types in Worker
    - 5\. Build Type Compatibility Map
    - 6\. Format Types
    - 7\. Group by Component
    - 8\. Deduplicate and Normalize
    - 9\. Detect Re-exports
    - 10\. Organize by Export
  - Worker Architecture
    - Main Thread Responsibilities
    - Worker Thread Responsibilities
    - Socket-Based IPC
  - Types
  - Related

## Load Server Types Text

A function for parsing `types.md` files back into structured type metadata. This is the reverse operation of [`syncTypes`](../sync-types/page.mdx) — it reads markdown and reconstructs organized type exports.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Usage
  - Markdown Format
    - Type Sections
    - Type Detection
    - Tables
    - External Types
    - Embedded Metadata
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - loadServerTypesText
    - Parameters: fileUrl, ordering
- Types: TypesSourceData

</details>

## Loader Utilities

The loader utilities provide a comprehensive set of functions for resolving, parsing, and processing import statements in JavaScript/TypeScript code. These utilities are essential for building systems that need to analyze and transform code imports, such as code highlighters, bundlers, and documentation generators.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Architecture
  - Core Functions

## Parse Create Factory Call

The `parseCreateFactoryCall` utility is a generic source-code parser for `createX(import.meta.url, …)`-style factory calls. Given the source of a file, it locates the first `createSomething(import.meta.url, …)` call, resolves any imported variants against the file's URL, and returns a structured result describing the variants, options, named exports, and surrounding imports.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Single component shorthand
    - Named exports
    - Factory options
    - Fetching demos from a remote source
    - Replacing precomputed values
  - How It Works
  - Types
    - `parseCreateFactoryCall`
    - `parseAllCreateFactoryCalls`
    - `replacePrecomputeValue`
  - Additional Types
- Exports:
  - parseAllCreateFactoryCalls
    - Parameters: code, filePath, parseOptions
  - parseCreateFactoryCall
    - Parameters: code, filePath, parseOptions, importsAndComments
  - replacePrecomputeValue
    - Parameters: source, precomputeData, demoCallInfo, options
- Types: FactoryOptions, ParseOptions, ParsedCreateFactory

</details>

## Parse Source

The `parseSource` utility parses source code into HAST (Hypertext Abstract Syntax Tree) nodes with syntax highlighting using [Starry Night](https://github.com/wooorm/starry-night). It converts code into highlighted HTML structures for display in documentation and demos.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Highlighting Different Languages
    - Processing Multiple Files
    - Unsupported File Types
  - Advanced Usage
    - Global Instance Pattern
    - Integration with Code Highlighter
  - How It Works
    - Syntax Token Classes
  - Supported Languages
  - Error Handling
    - Uninitialized Parser
    - Empty Content
  - When to Use
  - Types
    - `createParseSource`
    - `parseSource`
    - `getGrammarFromLanguage`
  - Additional Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createParseSource
  - getGrammarFromLanguage
    - Parameters: language
  - parseSource
    - Parameters: source, fileName, language
- Types: extensionMap, languageToGrammarMap

</details>

## Sync Page Index

The `syncPageIndex` function automatically maintains index pages by extracting metadata from documentation pages and updating parent directory indexes. It's designed to work with Next.js file-based routing, keeping navigation indexes in sync as pages are added or modified.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:

## Sync Types

The server-side function for synchronizing TypeScript type documentation to disk. This function coordinates type loading and markdown generation, updating the `types.md` file and optionally the parent index page.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Usage
    - Basic Usage
  - TypesMeta Structure
  - Processing Pipeline
    - 1\. Load and Format Types
    - 2\. Generate and Write Markdown
    - 3\. Update Parent Index (Optional)
  - Related
  - Types
- Exports:
  - syncTypes
    - Parameters: options
- Types: SyncTypesOptions

</details>

## Transform HTML Code Block

A rehype plugin that transforms `<pre>` elements containing `<code>` blocks into precomputed data for the CodeHighlighter component. This plugin extracts source code from HTML, processes it through syntax highlighting and transformations, then stores the results for efficient client-side rendering.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Key Features
  - Installation & Usage
  - Optional Configuration
    - With Next.js and MDX
  - Input Examples
    - Single Code Block
    - Multiple Code Variants (Different Languages)
    - Custom Variant Names
    - Custom Filenames
    - Code Block Transformations
  - Output
  - Data Attributes
    - Reserved Attributes (handled internally)
    - Custom Attributes (passed to content component)
  - Variant Naming
  - Supported Languages
  - Integration with transformMarkdownCodeVariants
    - Complete Example

</details>

## Transform HTML Code Inline

A rehype plugin that applies lightweight syntax highlighting to inline `<code>` elements without adding line gutters, frame wrappers, or precomputed data attributes.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Installation

    - With Highlighting Prefix

## Transform Markdown Blockquote Callouts

The `transformMarkdownBlockquoteCallouts` plugin is a Remark plugin that enables GitHub-style callout blocks in markdown. It transforms blockquotes with special markers (like `> [!NOTE]`) into HTML blockquotes with a `data-callout-type` attribute, making it easy to style and render callouts in your documentation.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Supported Callout Types
    - Default Colors
  - Example
    - How to Write a Callout in Markdown
    - Live Callout Examples
  - Usage
  - Integration
  - Best Practices
  - Related

</details>

## Transform Markdown Code

A remark plugin that transforms markdown code blocks into HTML structures with enhanced metadata support. This plugin handles both individual code blocks with options and multi-variant code examples. It's the **first stage** in a processing pipeline, typically followed by [`transformHtmlCodeBlock`](../transform-html-code-block/page.mdx) for final rendering.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Key Features
  - Installation & Usage
    - With Next.js and MDX
  - Syntax Examples
    - Individual Code Blocks with Options
    - Basic Variants (variant=name)
    - Labeled Variants (variant-group=name)
    - Different Languages
    - Custom Properties
  - Plugin Behavior
    - Individual Code Block Processing
    - Grouping Rules
    - Label Extraction (variant-group only)
    - Language Support

  - Error Handling

</details>

## Transform Markdown Meta Links

A remark plugin that automatically cleans up meta links ("[See Demo]", "[See Setup]", "[See Types]") and horizontal rules that follow Demo and Types components in markdown documentation. This plugin identifies these components (without `.Title`) and removes associated navigation elements to create cleaner documentation.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Key Features
  - Installation & Usage
    - With docs-infra
  - Syntax Examples
    - Basic Demo Component Cleanup
    - Demo Component with Properties
    - Horizontal Rule Only Cleanup
    - Types Component Cleanup
    - Setup Link Cleanup
  - Component Detection
    - Demo.Title Exclusion
  - Plugin Behavior
    - Processing Rules
    - Element Matching
    - Content Preservation
  - Common Use Cases
    - Documentation Cleanup
    - Migration from External Demos
    - Streamlined Reading Experience
  - Configuration
  - Troubleshooting
    - Links Not Being Removed
    - Components Not Detected
    - Unexpected Content Removal

</details>

## Transform Markdown Metadata

A remark plugin that extracts metadata from MDX files and optionally updates parent directory index pages. This plugin automatically collects page titles, descriptions, keywords, and hierarchical section structures to create searchable, navigable documentation indexes.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

## Transform Markdown Relative Paths

The `transformMarkdownRelativePaths` plugin is a Remark plugin that automatically transforms relative markdown links to work seamlessly in both development environments (VSCode, GitHub) and production builds. It strips page file extensions and converts relative paths to absolute URLs.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

## Transform Typescript To Javascript

The `transformTypescriptToJavascript` utility transforms TypeScript/TSX code into JavaScript/JSX using Babel, preserving formatting and automatically generating JavaScript variants for documentation and demos.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:

  - Usage

  - Example Transformations
  - When to Use
  - Related
  - Implementation Notes

[Read more](./transform-typescript-to-javascript/page.mdx)

## With Docs Infra

The `withDocsInfra` function is a Next.js configuration plugin that sets up webpack loaders, Turbopack rules, and page extensions required for MUI docs infrastructure. It automatically configures code highlighting loaders for demo files and provides sensible defaults for documentation sites.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Basic Usage
  - Advanced Configuration
  - Default Configurations
    - Page Extensions
    - Demo File Patterns
  - MDX Integration
    - Controlling Index Generation
  - Default MDX Plugins
    - Remark Plugins (Markdown processing)
    - Rehype Plugins (HTML processing)
    - Code Emphasis Configuration
  - Example: Custom Demo Structure
  - Auto-generating Demo `client.ts` Files
  - How It Works
  - Integration Example
  - Automatic Index Generation
  - When to Use
  - Types
    - `withDocsInfra`
    - `getDocsInfraMdxOptions`
    - `withDeploymentConfig`
  - Additional Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - getDocsInfraMdxOptions
    - Parameters: customOptions
  - withDeploymentConfig
    - Parameters: nextConfig
  - withDocsInfra
    - Parameters: options
- Types: DocsInfraMdxOptions, WebpackOptions, WithDocsInfraOptions

</details>
